My Story

It started on a cold December — the year my phone almost gave up on me.

By that point, I already had 3+ years of experience importing — regular items, the everyday hustle. But it was never enough to actually change anything. It just kept me getting by as a student.

So I made a decision. I was going to shift focus, learn how to import phones and laptops properly, and build something that could actually move my life forward.

December I'd already been building trust with my audience before I even started talking about this business. So the moment I posted about it, my first customer came in. A week later, the phone arrived. It felt unreal.
A Few Weeks Later My own phone finally broke down for real — turning on one moment, off the next. That same period, my second customer came in. I closed the sale on a Samsung S22+ I could barely use, and imported an iPhone 12 Pro Max for her.
From There One order became two. Two became three. Three became ten. And the business hasn't stopped moving since.

I'm not where I want to be yet. But this business can change your life. I spent 2+ years importing other things and never made, in that whole time, what I made in just 6 months importing gadgets.

I taught this exact skill to one of my mentees. Here's what happened next.

Who This Is For

This class was built for two kinds of people.

You already sell phones

You want to buy in bigger quantities, stop guessing on pricing, and actually start making real profit instead of just moving units.

You need your own income

You're a student (or anyone) tired of leaning fully on parents who are already stretched thin by this economy. This is a real way to start building something of your own.

What's Inside

Exactly what I'll show you, step by step.

  • ✓ How to make payments yourself, safely, without a middleman
  • ✓ How to get your goods from China down to Nigeria, step by step
  • ✓ How to do real market research so you know exactly what to charge
  • ✓ How to build trust with your audience so people buy from you on sight
  • ✓ How to scale — turning one order into ten, the way I did
